title: Small Vocabulary: Long Sequences
concept: A small vocabulary (e.g. 30,000 tokens) keeps the model lightweight but breaks words into many small fragments, increasing sequence length and compute.

exercises.prompt: What does an AI's 'vocabulary size' represent?

options.text: The set of words and word-parts (tokens) the AI knows.
The physical hard drive inside the AI server.
The list of programming languages the AI can write.

exercises.explain: A vocabulary in AI refers to the set of words and word-parts (tokens) that the model is trained to recognize and use.

cards.title: Small Vocabulary, Big Effort
cards.body: If an AI has a tiny vocabulary, it's like speaking using only basic ingredients. To say something fancy, you have to spell it out step-by-step. Let's see how a small vocabulary makes AI sequences super long and computationally expensive!

cards.title: Small Vocabulary, Long Sequences
cards.body: A small vocabulary (like 30,000 words or word-parts) keeps the AI's file size lightweight and easy to load. However, to write complex sentences, it has to chop words into tiny fragments (like 'un', 'stop', 'pable'). This makes the sequence of tokens very long, requiring more calculation steps to process.

cards.title: The Tiny Sock Suitcase
cards.body: Imagine packing a suitcase with tiny socks instead of large winter sweaters. Because each sock is so small, you need to pack hundreds of them to fill the bag. Your vocabulary size is like the size of the items you choose to pack. Packing tiny items (small vocabulary) means you have to pack a massive number of individual pieces (long sequence) to get the job done!

cards.title: Breaking Words Apart
cards.body: If an AI has a small vocabulary, it might not know the word "biodegradable". Instead of reading it as 1 token, it splits it into: "bio", "de", "grad", and "able" (4 tokens). A short paragraph of 100 words can easily balloon into 300 tokens, forcing the AI's processor to work three times harder!

cards.title: Fewer Words = Less Work?
cards.body: It's easy to assume that a smaller vocabulary always makes an AI faster because it has fewer words to remember. While it saves memory, the resulting long sequences actually make the AI do much more computational work to process a single page of text!

cards.title: Visualizing Vocabulary Size
cards.body: Compare how a small vocabulary changes the sequence size compared to a large vocabulary for the word 'incompatibility'.

left.text: in - comp - at - i - bil - i - ty (7 tokens to process)

exercises.prompt: What happens to sequence length and compute when an AI uses a very small vocabulary?

options.text: The sequences become longer, requiring more compute to process.
The AI instantly memorizes entire dictionaries.
The sequence length decreases to zero.
The AI stops using tokens altogether.

exercises.explain: When vocabulary is small, words must be broken down into many tiny pieces (tokens). This increases the length of the sequence, requiring more computational steps.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A small vocabulary generally leads to longer token sequences for the same text.
exercises.explain: A small vocabulary requires more tokens to represent the same text because words are split into smaller pieces, which increases the sequence length.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the explanation.
exercises.sentence: Using a small vocabulary means the AI must chop words into tiny fragments, creating longer sequences.
exercises.explain: A small vocabulary forces the AI to break words into small fragments, which increases the total token count.

exercises.prompt: Which AI assistant is giving incorrect information about vocabulary size?

options.text: AI A: "A small vocabulary keeps the model lightweight, but it splits words into many tokens, increasing sequence length."
AI B: "Because words are broken down into small fragments with a tiny vocabulary, the AI needs more compute to process them."
AI C: "A small vocabulary is great because it combines multiple sentences into single tokens, making sequences much shorter."

exercises.explain: The third option is generated by an AI that incorrectly claims a small vocabulary makes sequences shorter. In reality, it makes them longer because words are split into more pieces.

exercises.prompt: Your AI project uses a very small vocabulary size of 10,000 tokens. What is the main challenge your AI will face when reading a long book?

options.text: It makes the sequence of tokens much shorter and faster to read.
It forces the model to store billions of whole words in its memory.
It chops words into tiny pieces, creating long sequences that require more computing power.
It prevents the AI from learning any punctuation marks at all.

exercises.explain: A small vocabulary means words are chopped into many tiny pieces. This creates a very long sequence of tokens that the AI must process, which slows down its thinking and uses more computer energy (compute).
